message:
fix(btree): freeblock ordering and coalescing in deferred table leaf delete

The deferred delete path (remove_table_cell_from_leaf_deferred) was
prepending freed cells to the freeblock chain without maintaining
ascending offset order or coalescing adjacent freeblocks. C sqlite3's
btreeComputeFreeSpace() enforces three invariants that were violated:

1. Freeblocks must be chained in strictly ascending offset order.
2. Adjacent freeblocks (gap <= 3 bytes) must be merged into one.
3. The first freeblock must be at offset >= cell_content_offset.

When multiple adjacent cells were deleted (e.g. DELETE WHERE id BETWEEN
20 AND 60), the resulting freeblock chain contained adjacent entries that
failed check #2, causing C sqlite3's PRAGMA integrity_check to report
"free space corruption" even though frankensqlite's own checks passed.

The fix extracts freeblock insertion into insert_freeblock_sorted_coalesced()
which walks the chain to find the correct sorted position and merges with
the previous and/or next freeblock when they are physically adjacent.

Also fixes a pre-existing test (test_table_delete_reclaims_dead_space_on_
next_insert_rewrite) that used a rightmost rowid for reinsertion, which
triggered the append-on-leaf fast path that bypasses freeblock scanning.
Changed to reinsert at the deleted rowid to exercise the freeblock reuse
path as intended.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
